etymology.description: 兵(兵) (bīng, "soldier; weapon") refers to soldiers or warfare, encompassing everything related to military matters. 法(法) (fǎ, "method; rule; law") denotes a method or doctrine, indicating a set of structured practices or principles.
etymology.interpretation: 兵(兵) (bīng, "soldier; weapon") implies the entire scope of military operations, while 法(法) (fǎ, "method; rule; law") suggests a formalized system or set of techniques. Together, they signify the structured methods and principles essential to conducting warfare, encapsulating the strategic and tactical doctrines that guide military operations.

examples.chinese: 孙子兵法是一本著名的古代军事著作(孫子兵法是一本著名的古代軍事著作)。
examples.pinyin: Sūnzǐ Bīngfǎ shì yī běn zhùmíng de gǔdài jūnshì zhùzuò.
examples.english: The Art of War by Sun Tzu is a famous ancient military work.

examples.chinese: 他精通兵法(他精通兵法)，可以在战场上灵活应对(可以在戰場上靈活應對)。
examples.pinyin: Tā jīngtōng bīngfǎ, kěyǐ zài zhànchǎng shàng línghuó yìngduì.
examples.english: He is well-versed in military methods and can adapt flexibly on the battlefield.

examples.chinese: 了解各种兵法(了解各種兵法)，有助于提高战略思维(有助於提高戰略思維)。
examples.pinyin: Liǎojiě gèzhǒng bīngfǎ, yǒuzhù yú tígāo zhànlüè sīwéi.
examples.english: Understanding various military methods helps in enhancing strategic thinking.
